the general solution for p(t) is found by solving the differential equation, which involves integrating the terms rP(1 - P/K) and A sin(2πt/12)P. the specific solution depends on the initial condition p(0) = p0, and the behavior of the sinusoidal term affects the oscillations of the population. the sinusoidal term, with amplitude a = 0.1, influences the timing of the peaks, causing them to shift slightly over time. the network of data points and the money involved in the study help to understand the dynamics of the beetle population. the travel required for the study adds to the complexity of the project.
